QF.id: VII Market Participants in Securities Markets_33

question.text: As per SEBI regulations, a custodian must open what type of account for each client?

answer.text: A separate custody account in the name of each client, with assets not mixed between clients
A single pooled account for all clients
A joint account with the custodian as co-holder
An account in the custodian's own name with sub-ledgers

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Every custodian must open a separate custody account for each client, in the name of the client whose securities are in custody. Assets of one client shall NOT be mixed with those of another client. This ensures segregation and protection of client assets. **The Trap:** Unlike a broker's pool account, custodian accounts are strictly client-name and segregated. No mixing, no pooling, no co-holding. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VI covers account segregation requirements for depository participants. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Custodian golden rule: segregation at all levels. Separate accounts per client, separate from custodian's own assets, separate from other businesses. Internal controls must prevent manipulation of records.
